preamble: The shift from the voiced labiodental fricative [v] to the voiced dental fricative [ð] (represented here as 'th_vced') is documented in the historical development of English and as a dialectal correspondence in varieties featuring Th-fronting.

examples.note: Historical development where labiodental fricatives in specific environments (e.g., before -er) shifted toward dental articulations, or vice-versa in dialects.

While the shift is typically dental to labiodental (mother > muvver), the acoustic proximity leads to regular correspondences and occasional reverse substitutions in hyper-correction or specific phonetic environments.

sources: Phonological history of English consonants (Middle English transitions).
